ocr.text: The dynamics of dry bulk commodity shipping continue to evolve, reflecting broader economic trends and market demands
throughout Europe. As illustrated in the accompanying data table, Hapag-Lloyd has made significant strides in its shipping
operations, particularly in the transportation of key raw materials such as coal. The table highlights the volume of UnitsSold,
revealing that in 2021, the UnitsSold reached a substantial CHF100M, underscoring the company’s robust engagement in this
sector during a pivotal year for the industry. The percentage change in coal volumes has been notable, with a fluctuation that
resulted in a PercentageChange of CHF120M, indicating the volatility inherent in commodity markets and the impacts of

geopolitical factors. 

Moreover, projections for future performance suggest a continued emphasis on coal, with expectations that
UnitsSold will increase by 6% over the coming periods.
CommodityType  Year  VolumeinTons
UnitsSold  CHF  PercentageChange  Coal  2020  2021  2022  UnitsSold
15%  Grains  2020  2021  2022  UnitsSold  1,500,000  CHF150M
[HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  12%  1,000,000  [HIDDEN]  8%  Total
This anticipated growth aligns with ongoing global energy demands and the gradual recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic,
which had previously disrupted supply chains and shipping schedules. Notably, the financial implications of these shipping
volumes are significant, as evidenced by the overall revenue generation that is expected to reach CHF1,200,000 by the end of

the current reporting period. 

In summary, Hapag-Lloyd’s strategic focus on dry bulk commodities, particularly coal, is set
against a backdrop of fluctuating market conditions and evolving energy needs across Europe. The data presented not only
reflects past performance but also serves as a critical indicator of future trends in the dry bulk shipping sector. As markets
stabilize and demand for essential commodities rises, the company's operational agility and responsiveness to market changes
will be paramount in capitalizing on emerging opportunities. Thus, stakeholders can expect Hapag-Lloyd to maintain its
position as a key player in the European maritime landscape, adeptly navigating the complexities of dry bulk shipping in the
years to come.
